skill.name: performance-profiling
skill.description: Performance profiling guidance for measuring web speed, finding bottlenecks, and validating targeted optimizations.
skill.summary: Measure Core Web Vitals, analyze bottlenecks, and validate performance improvements.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found external command, network, filesystem, and weak cryptography patterns. Review confirmed legitimate Lighthouse profiling behavior with medium risk from running an external CLI against user-supplied URLs; markdown backticks, example URLs, and the weak cryptography flag are false positives.

medium_findings.title: External Lighthouse Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The script invokes the hardcoded Lighthouse CLI with subprocess.run and shell execution disabled. This appears legitimate for profiling, but it still runs local external software and depends on the installed Lighthouse binary.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is directly present and semantically tied to the skill purpose. The hardcoded executable and shell=False behavior reduce command injection risk.

medium_findings.title: User-Supplied URL Network Access
medium_findings.description: The script passes the command-line URL into Lighthouse, causing browser-based network requests to the supplied target. This is expected for audits, but it can reach internal or sensitive URLs if a user provides them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL argument is passed directly into the Lighthouse command. The behavior is legitimate for performance testing, but the network target is user controlled.

low_findings.title: Temporary Lighthouse Report File
low_findings.description: The script creates a temporary JSON report and deletes it after parsing. If Lighthouse or parsing fails before cleanup, a local report file may remain.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The temp file lifecycle is visible in the script. The risk is low because the file contains Lighthouse output and is normally removed.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged markdown backticks in SKILL.md as shell execution. These lines are documentation examples and workflow text, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is inside a markdown table and a fenced workflow block. No executable code is present at those locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Example URL
low_findings.description: The hardcoded https://example.com values are usage examples in documentation and script help text. No evidence found that the skill sends data to that domain.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both instances are user-facing usage examples. The active network target comes from the user-provided URL, not a hidden hardcoded endpoint.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 3, but that line is only the skill description. No evidence found of cryptographic code or weak cryptographic algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains descriptive text only. The reviewed files do not show hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API use.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run Invokes External CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The script runs Lighthouse through subprocess.run. Arguments are passed as a list with no shell, so command injection risk is reduced, but external execution remains a publish-time risk factor.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact external command pattern is present. The surrounding context supports a legitimate audit use case rather than malicious execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Arbitrary Audit Target URL
dangerous_patterns.description: The audit target is taken from the command line and supplied to Lighthouse. Users should avoid running it against internal services or targets they are not authorized to test.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The user-controlled URL path is clear from the script entry point to the Lighthouse arguments. This is normal for the skill, but still requires user caution.

content.value_statement: Slow web experiences reduce engagement and make optimization decisions unclear. This skill guides measurement, bottleneck analysis, Lighthouse audits, and validation steps.
actual_capabilities: Explains Core Web Vitals targets for loading, interactivity, and layout stability.
Provides a measure, identify, fix, and validate workflow for performance work.
Maps common symptoms to profiling tools such as Lighthouse and DevTools.
Guides bundle analysis for large dependencies, duplicate code, and missing splits.
Includes a Lighthouse helper script that summarizes category scores for a URL.
Lists quick optimization priorities such as compression, lazy loading, and caching.
limitations: The helper script requires the Lighthouse CLI to be installed separately.
The Lighthouse script makes network requests to the URL supplied by the user.
It provides guidance and basic audit output, not continuous production monitoring.
It does not replace detailed application-specific profiling or load testing.

use_cases.title: Diagnose a Slow Landing Page
use_cases.description: Run a structured audit and identify whether loading, JavaScript, images, or caching cause the main delay.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Performance Fix Plan
use_cases.description: Turn profiling results into prioritized changes with a clear baseline and validation method.

use_cases.title: Review Release Readiness
use_cases.description: Check Core Web Vitals, bundle concerns, and Lighthouse scores before shipping a web release.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Performance Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the performance-profiling skill to review my web page performance. Help me define a baseline and choose the first tools to run.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Lighthouse Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the performance-profiling skill to interpret these Lighthouse results. Identify the main bottlenecks and suggest the highest-impact fixes first.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Bundle Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the performance-profiling skill to analyze my bundle report. Find large dependencies, duplicate code, unused code, and code-splitting opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Validation Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the performance-profiling skill to create a validation plan for this performance change. Include baseline metrics, target metrics, test steps, and rollback signals.

output_examples.input: Review a page with poor Lighthouse performance and high LCP.
output: Baseline: Capture Lighthouse and field data before changing code.
Likely focus: Reduce render-blocking assets, optimize hero image delivery, and check server response time.
Validation: Re-run Lighthouse and compare LCP, total blocking time, and visual stability.

output_examples.input: Find quick wins for a JavaScript-heavy route.
output: Check the bundle analyzer for large libraries and duplicate dependencies.
Split route-level code so unused screens do not load during the initial visit.
Validate with DevTools coverage and a new Lighthouse run.

output_examples.input: Create a performance investigation workflow for a release candidate.
output: Measure current Core Web Vitals and Lighthouse category scores.
Profile runtime interactions for long tasks and layout work.
Document fixes, compare results, and keep the changes that improve user-facing metrics.

best_practices: Measure before changing code, then validate every optimization against the same baseline.
Prioritize user-visible bottlenecks such as loading, responsiveness, and layout stability.
Use authorized targets only when running Lighthouse or other network-based audits.
anti_patterns: Do not optimize from guesses without profiling data.
Do not focus on tiny code changes while large assets or blocking scripts dominate load time.
Do not run audits against private or third-party systems without permission.

faq.question: What does this skill help measure?
faq.answer: It helps measure page load, Core Web Vitals, bundle size, runtime work, memory symptoms, and Lighthouse category scores.

faq.question: Does it include an executable script?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Python helper that runs the Lighthouse CLI against a user-supplied URL.

faq.question: Is the Lighthouse script required?
faq.answer: No. The written workflow can be used without the script, but automated scores require the Lighthouse CLI.

faq.question: Can it optimize my code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides investigation and prioritization. Code changes still depend on the application and profiling evidence.

faq.question: What should I check before running the script?
faq.answer: Confirm Lighthouse is installed and only audit URLs you own or have permission to test.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
